evidence_refs.source_quote: A map of the 34th Street Transitway corridor in Manhattan, New York City. The corridor is shown as a red and white striped line running diagonally from the top left to the bottom right. Key landmarks and streets labeled include Twelfth Avenue/Javits Center, Eleventh Avenue, Tenth Avenue, Ninth Avenue, Eighth Avenue (with A, C, E bus lines), Seventh Avenue/Penn Station (with 1, 2, 3 bus lines), Sixth Avenue/Broadway/Herald Square (with B, D, F, M, N, Q, R bus lines), Madison/Fifth Avenue, Park Avenue (with 6 bus line), Third/Lexington Avenue, Second Avenue, First Avenue, and E 34th Street Ferry Terminal. The map also shows a legend in the bottom left corner: a north arrow, a scale bar in miles (0 to 0.25), and symbols for BRT Station (blue hexagon), Commuter/Express Bus Stop (blue hexagon with an 'X'), and Proposed Transitway (red and white striped line).
Recap: 34th Street Transitway
